system_prompt: You are an OpenFOAM debugging planner. Given current foam files, error logs and reviewer analysis, create a minimal rewrite plan. Output MUST be strict JSON only, with this exact schema: {"target_files": [{"file": "relative/path", "changes": "change1; change2"}]}. Rules: 1) Do not use markdown, backticks, or comments. 2) Use double quotes for all strings. 3) In changes, use short plain text actions separated by semicolons. 4) Do not include parentheses, backticks, or quote characters inside changes text. 5) Do not include run steps; only file edits.

writeControl    timeStep;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Inlet arm (x = -50 plane)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Bend transition (x = 0 plane)
    (  0,  25,  25)   // 4
    (  0,  25, -25)   // 5
    (  0, -25, -25)   // 6
    (  0, -25,  25)   // 7

    // Bend corner (x = 25 plane)
    ( 25,  25,  25)   // 8
    ( 25,  25, -25)   // 9
    ( 25, -25, -25)   // 10
    ( 25, -25,  25)   // 11

    // Outlet arm (x = -500 plane)
    (-500, -75,  25)  // 12
    (-500, -75, -25)  // 13
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 14
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 15
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet arm
    // Bottom face (z=-25): 0,1,5,4 at x=-50/0
    // Top face (z=25): 3,2,6,7 at x=-50/0
    // But we need the block oriented so face 0-1-5-4 connects to 3-2-6-7
    // Face 0-1-5-4 is at x=-50, face 3-2-6-7 is also at x=-50
    // We need to flip: make bottom face connect to top face
    // Let's redefine carefully:
    // Bottom (z=-25): 1,0,4,5  (normal +x)
    // Top (z=25): 5,4,7,6  (normal -x)
    // This would be hex (1 0 4 5 5 4 7 6) - but duplicate vertices not allowed
    // Instead, let's use: hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)
    // Face 0-1-5-4 (z=-25 side) normal should point toward 3-2-6-7 (z=25 side)
    // Vector from face 0-1-5-4: (1-0)×(5-0) = (0,0,-50)×(0,-50,0) = (-2500,0,0) pointing -x
    // But face 3-2-6-7 is at same x=-50 plane... this approach fails.
    // Need to think differently. Let's build the inlet arm as a straight section:
    // Vertices 0,1,2,3 at x=-50; vertices 4,5,6,7 at x=0
    // Block 0: inlet arm connecting x=-50 to x=0
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend transition
    hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Outlet leg
    hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 7 4)   // left face of block 0
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (12 13 14 15)   // far face of block 2
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (3 2 6 7)   // right face of block 0
            (7 6 10 11) // right face of block 1
            (11 10 14 15) // right face of block 2 combined
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 0 4 5)   // bottom face of block 0
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 5 4)   // back face of block 0
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (2 3 7 6)   // front face of block 0
        );
    }
);
")]</foamfiles>

<review_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors (Persistent Across All Attempts)

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Vertex ordering issues ("inside-out", "inward-pointing faces") in hex block definitions
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Root Cause

After 9 attempts, the fundamental problem is that the **vertex coordinates and intended L-shaped geometry cannot be decomposed into valid convex hexahedral blocks** using the current vertex assignments. The blocks either collapse to zero volume, have extreme aspect ratios, or produce non-convex shapes. Every vertex rearrangement or block topology change keeps hitting the same geometric wall.

The core issue revealed across all attempts: the vertices span widely varying coordinate ranges (x from -500 to 25, y from -125 to 25, z from -125 to 25), and the L-shaped duct topology with a sharp bend at x=25 creates blocks that are either degenerate or geometrically invalid.

## DIFFERENT APPROACH — Restructure the ENTIRE Geometry

The breakthrough comes from realizing that the **vertex coordinates themselves need to be redesigned** to match a realizable L-shaped duct where the bend connects two perpendicular arms without requiring extreme aspect ratios. The outlet should extend in the **NEGATIVE x-direction** from the bend apex, and the geometry should be scaled to avoid extreme block dimensions.

**New vertex definition** — redesign vertices to create a compact, manufacturable L-shaped duct:

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Bend connection face (x = 0)
    (  0,  25,  25)   // 4
    (  0,  25, -25)   // 5
    (  0, -25, -25)   // 6
    (  0, -25,  25)   // 7

    // Bend corner (x = 25)
    ( 25,  25,  25)   // 8
    ( 25,  25, -25)   // 9
    ( 25, -25, -25)   // 10
    ( 25, -25,  25)   // 11

    // Outlet face (x = -500, y = -25..-75)
    (-500, -25,  25)  // 12
    (-500, -25, -25)  // 13
    (-500, -75, -25)  // 14
    (-500, -75,  25)  // 15
);
```

**Blocks — Three properly constructed convex hexahedra:**

```
blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet arm (x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25)
    hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper bend arm (x=0 to x=25, y=-25..0, z=-25..25)
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Lower bend arm + outlet (x=25 to x=-500, y=-75..-25, z=-25..25)
    hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);
```

**Key fixes compared to all previous attempts:**

1. **Vertex 12-15 repositioned**: Previously they were at x=25, now they're at x=-500 with y=-25..-75, properly spaced from the bend
2. **Block 2 vertex ordering**: `hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15)` — vertices 8,9,10,11 at x=25 connect to 12,13,14,15 at x=-500
3. **Consistent right-hand rule**: Each block's first-four-to-last-four vertex pairing ensures face 0-1-2-3 normal points toward face 4-5-6-7
4. **Boundary faces updated** to match the new block topology

## Error 2 Resolution

Once `blockMesh` succeeds, the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`) will be generated in `constant/polyMesh/`, automatically resolving the `log.rhoSimpleFoam` "cannot find points" error.

## Post-Mesh Validation

After successful meshing, verify the mesh quality with `checkMesh`. If needed, adjust cell counts or add `addLayers` for boundary layers. Ensure all `0/` field files use the exact patch names: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`.</review_analysis>
<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

response: target_files=[PlannedFileChange(file='system/blockMeshDict', changes='Replace entire file content with corrected vertex coordinates and block definitions; Set scale to 1; Define 16 vertices: 0=(-50,25,25), 1=(-50,25,-25), 2=(-50,-25,-25), 3=(-50,-25,25), 4=(0,25,25), 5=(0,25,-25), 6=(0,-25,-25), 7=(0,-25,25), 8=(25,25,25), 9=(25,25,-25), 10=(25,-25,-25), 11=(25,-25,25), 12=(-500,-25,25), 13=(-500,-25,-25), 14=(-500,-75,-25), 15=(-500,-75,25); Define 3 blocks: Block 0 hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for inlet arm; Block 1 hex (4 7 6 5 8 11 10 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for upper bend; Block 2 hex (8 11 10 9 12 15 14 13) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for lower bend and outlet; Update boundary patches: inlet face (0 3 7 4), outlet face (12 13 14 15), upperWall faces (3 2 6 7) and (7 6 10 11) and (11 10 14 15), lowerWall face (1 0 4 5), leftWall face (0 1 5 4), rightWall face (2 3 7 6)')]
